growth_records.strain: Fusaro (model iAF692); physiological uptake/yield values compiled from cited M. barkeri growth studies
growth_records.medium: Methanogenic minimal medium (methanol as primary substrate); model minimal medium per Wolin et al. 1963

up.method: reported (maximum specific uptake rate observed for M. barkeri on methanol, taken directly or approximated from biomass yields and maximal specific growth rates in Elferink et al. 1994; used as model constraint)

sec.method: reported yield only; experimentally observed growth yield on methanol = 5-7 gDW/mol CH4 (Smith & Mah 1978; Weimer & Zeikus 1978; Hippe et al. 1979). Absolute CH4 secretion flux not stated.
product of methanol oxidation branch; absolute flux not reported (only in supplementary FBA flux distribution files)

growth_records.cite: Feist et al. Modeling methanogenesis with a genome-scale metabolic reconstruction of Methanosarcina barkeri. Molecular Systems Biology (2006). DOI:10.1038/msb4100046. [PMC1681478]
growth_records.snippet: 16 mmol methanol gDW-1 h-1 and 8 mmol acetate gDW-1 h-1 (Elferink et al, 1994) ... The observed growth yields ... on methanol (5-7 gDW mol-1 CH4)
growth_records.notes: Values are experimentally observed maximum specific substrate uptake rates and biomass-per-methane yields for M. barkeri, compiled in this paper's Methods from cited primary growth studies and used as constraints for the iAF692 model; they are physiological (not FBA-simulated). Methanol qS = 16 mmol/gDW/h (Elferink et al. 1994). Observed methanol growth yield 5-7 gDW/mol CH4. Growth temperature/pH not stated in main text (intracellular pH 7.2 was used for metabolite charge assignment, not medium pH). Bomar et al. 1985 diazotrophic vs NH4 methanol growth also used methanol uptake constrained at 16 mmol/gDW/h; N content 0.069 (N2) and 0.066 (NH4) gN/gDW; methanol assimilated 43% (N2) vs 56% (NH4). No absolute mu given in this paper.

growth_records.strain: Fusaro (model iAF692); physiological uptake/yield values compiled from cited M. barkeri growth studies
growth_records.medium: Methanogenic minimal medium (acetate as primary substrate)

up.method: reported (maximum specific uptake rate observed for M. barkeri on acetate; Elferink et al. 1994; used as model constraint)

sec.method: reported yield only; observed growth yield on acetate = 2-4 gDW/mol CH4 (Smith & Mah 1978; Bock et al. 1994). Absolute CH4 flux not stated.
acetoclastic methanogenesis co-product; absolute flux not reported

growth_records.cite: Feist et al. Modeling methanogenesis with a genome-scale metabolic reconstruction of Methanosarcina barkeri. Molecular Systems Biology (2006). DOI:10.1038/msb4100046. [PMC1681478]
growth_records.snippet: 8 mmol acetate gDW-1 h-1 (Elferink et al, 1994) ... acetate (2-4 gDW mol-1 CH4) (Smith and Mah, 1978; Bock et al, 1994)
growth_records.notes: Acetate qS = 8 mmol/gDW/h, experimentally observed maximum specific uptake rate for M. barkeri (Elferink et al. 1994) compiled in Methods and used as iAF692 constraint. Observed acetoclastic growth yield 2-4 gDW/mol CH4. No mu, temperature or pH stated in this paper.

growth_records.strain: Fusaro (model iAF692); physiological uptake/yield values compiled from cited M. barkeri growth studies
growth_records.medium: Methanogenic minimal medium (H2/CO2 80:20 v/v as primary substrate)

up.method: reported (maximum specific H2 uptake rate observed for M. barkeri; Smith & Mah 1978; used as model constraint)
co-substrate; uptake not constrained (the amount of CO2 was not constrained)

sec.method: reported yield only; observed growth yield on H2/CO2 = 6-9 gDW/mol CH4 (Smith & Mah 1978; Weimer & Zeikus 1978). Absolute CH4 flux not stated.

growth_records.cite: Feist et al. Modeling methanogenesis with a genome-scale metabolic reconstruction of Methanosarcina barkeri. Molecular Systems Biology (2006). DOI:10.1038/msb4100046. [PMC1681478]
growth_records.snippet: 41 mmol H2 gDW-1 h-1 (Smith and Mah, 1978) (the amount of CO2 was not constrained) ... H2/CO2 (80:20 v/v) (6-9 gDW mol-1 CH4)
growth_records.notes: H2 qS = 41 mmol/gDW/h, experimentally observed maximum specific uptake rate (Smith & Mah 1978); CO2 co-substrate uptake explicitly not constrained. Observed hydrogenotrophic growth yield 6-9 gDW/mol CH4. No mu, temperature or pH stated in this paper.

growth_records.strain: Fusaro (model iAF692); pyruvate physiology from isolated M. barkeri strain, Bock et al. 1994
growth_records.medium: Methanogenic minimal medium (pyruvate as primary substrate)

up.method: reported (maximum specific pyruvate uptake rate observed for M. barkeri; Bock et al. 1994; used as model constraint)

sec.method: reported yield only; observed growth yield on pyruvate = 12-15 gDW/mol CH4 (Bock et al. 1994). Absolute CH4 flux not stated.

growth_records.cite: Feist et al. Modeling methanogenesis with a genome-scale metabolic reconstruction of Methanosarcina barkeri. Molecular Systems Biology (2006). DOI:10.1038/msb4100046. [PMC1681478]
growth_records.snippet: 5 mmol pyruvate gDW-1 h-1 (Bock et al, 1994) ... pyruvate (12-15 gDW mol-1 CH4) (Bock et al, 1994)
growth_records.notes: Pyruvate qS = 5 mmol/gDW/h, experimentally observed maximum specific uptake rate for an isolated M. barkeri strain (Bock et al. 1994) compiled in Methods and used as iAF692 constraint. Observed growth yield 12-15 gDW/mol CH4 (highest of the four substrates). No mu, temperature or pH stated in this paper.

up.method: derived from CH4 Vmax via stoichiometry (whole-cell suspension, Michaelis-Menten)

up.units: umol consumed per bottle over 6 days (30 ml, batch)

sec.method: whole-cell suspension assay, GC-FID, Michaelis-Menten fit

sec.units: umol produced per bottle over 6 days (batch)
umol per bottle (derived: 1 CO2 per 3 CH4 from 4 MeOH->3CH4+CO2+2H2O)

growth_records.cite: Yin, Yongchao et al. Nitrous oxide inhibition of methanogenesis represents an underappreciated greenhouse gas emission feedback. The ISME Journal (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrae027. [PMC10960958]
growth_records.snippet: MeOH-grown M. barkeri cultures consumed 895 +/- 10 umol of MeOH within a 6-day incubation period and produced 635 +/- 34 umol of CH4 ... Vmax ... 440.4 +/- 10.2 nmol CH4 min-1 mg protein-1 ... Km 2.1(+/-0.1)x10^3 uM ... KI 130.9 +/- 4.7 uM
growth_records.notes: Methylotrophic methanogenesis, Eq.1: 4CH3OH->3CH4+CO2+2H2O (dG=-105 kJ/mol CH4). Measured Vmax(CH4)=440.4 nmol/min/mg protein at 0 uM N2O (Table 1). Km(MeOH)=2.1e3 uM. Inhibitory constant KI(N2O)=130.9+/-4.7 uM. Derived MeOH uptake at Vmax = 440.4 x 4/3 = 587.2 nmol/min/mg (stoich 4 MeOH:3 CH4). Bulk batch: 895 umol MeOH consumed, 635 umol CH4 produced in 6 days (30 ml). Growth yield 5.3e6 cells/umol CH4 = 3.57 ug biomass/umol CH4 (Table 2). N2O-inhibited Vmax: 244.1(100uM), 188.8(200uM); N2O-inhibited yields: 1.30(100uM), 0.62(200uM) ug/umol. M. barkeri has no nos operon (does not consume N2O).

up.method: derived from CH4 Vmax x4 stoichiometry (whole-cell suspension)

up.units: umol H2 supplied per bottle (1.24 mmol), consumed over 11 days batch

sec.method: whole-cell suspension assay, GC-FID, Michaelis-Menten fit

sec.units: umol produced per bottle over 11 days (batch)

yields.note: ~10-fold lower than literature range 5.4-8.6; thermodynamic prediction 3.6

growth_records.cite: Yin, Yongchao et al. Nitrous oxide inhibition of methanogenesis represents an underappreciated greenhouse gas emission feedback. The ISME Journal (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrae027. [PMC10960958]
growth_records.snippet: M. barkeri cultures, that received H2 as electron donor, produced 318 +/- 21 umol of CH4 from 1.24 mmol of H2 over an 11-day incubation period ... Vmax ... 138.1 +/- 6.9 nmol CH4 min-1 mg protein-1 ... Km 51.1 +/- 7.2 uM ... KI 90.6 +/- 10.
growth_records.notes: Hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis, Eq.2: 4H2+CO2->CH4+2H2O (dG=-131 kJ/mol CH4). Measured Vmax(CH4)=138.1 nmol/min/mg protein at 0 uM N2O. Km(H2)=51.1 uM. KI(N2O)=90.6+/-10.8 uM. Derived H2 uptake at Vmax = 138.1 x 4 = 552.4 nmol/min/mg (stoich 4 H2:1 CH4). Bulk batch: 1.24 mmol H2 -> 318 umol CH4 in 11 days (expected 310 umol). Growth yield 0.8e6 cells/umol CH4 = 0.53 ug biomass/umol CH4; authors note this is ~10x lower than literature for H2/CO2. N2O-inhibited Vmax: 82.8(50uM), 59.9(100uM); yields 0.15(50uM), 0.08(100uM).

up.units: umol consumed per bottle over 38 days (batch)

sec.method: whole-cell suspension assay, GC-FID, Michaelis-Menten fit

sec.units: umol produced per bottle over 38 days (batch)
umol per bottle (derived 1:1, CH3COO- + H+ -> CH4 + CO2)

growth_records.cite: Yin, Yongchao et al. Nitrous oxide inhibition of methanogenesis represents an underappreciated greenhouse gas emission feedback. The ISME Journal (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrae027. [PMC10960958]
growth_records.snippet: M. barkeri produced 588 +/- 24 umol of CH4 from 605 +/- 17 umol of acetate over a 38-day incubation period ... Vmax ... 40.8 +/- 4.7 nmol CH4 min-1 mg protein-1 ... Km 13.4(+/-2.7)x10^3 uM ... KI 24.8 +/- 3.1 uM
growth_records.notes: Acetoclastic methanogenesis, Eq.3: CH3COO- + H+ -> CH4 + CO2 (dG'=-35 kJ/mol CH4). Measured Vmax(CH4)=40.8 nmol/min/mg protein at 0 uM N2O. Km(acetate)=13.4e3 uM. KI(N2O)=24.8+/-3.1 uM (most N2O-sensitive pathway). Acetate uptake = CH4 Vmax (1:1) = 40.8 nmol/min/mg. Bulk batch: 605 umol acetate -> 588 umol CH4 in 38 days. Growth yield 2.1e6 cells/umol CH4 = 1.45 ug biomass/umol CH4. N2O-inhibited Vmax: 32.1(20uM), 22.8(40-50uM); yields 1.02(20uM), 0.70(50uM).
